embeds.title: The Alchemist (short story)
embeds.description: "The Alchemist" is a short story by American writer H. P. Lovecraft. It was written in 1908, when Lovecraft was 17 or 18, and first published in the November 1916 issue of the United Amateur.

content: also oh i found the bit I was thinking of
this took me legit like a full hour to figure out how I wanted to word this
in fact it took so much effort I kinda stopped afterwards
...i hate to be this guy but should it be king's oracles?
tbh im a bit surprised his name was never changed to Enoch or something else
the king hears the god's oracles (oracles in this context being used in the noun form, in the meaning of "communication from god")
you might be thinking of oracles as a title which is more common because of the prevalence of greek mythology where it's the title of a person who speaks god's words
yeah, I've heard of it as oracle (person) and oracle (their prediction/knowledge) but I've never seen oracles used for plural prophecies
it's a fairly archaic usage of the word which is also why I chose it over prophecies because it's written in a pretty archaic way
that was one of the things that took so long to consider
because I was like "should I just use revelation? people would understand it a lot better if I just used revelation"
it works I guess i'm just curious if it's like closer to the meaning than prophecies would be since i think prophecies would be like. a clearer read I guess?
"but also like the whole point is that this is religious-y sacramental speech and needs to be archaic and fancy because he's describing the religious history of their nation so like"
words are hard and like I *should* just be churning out a first pass of the story in a few days and then doing a more detailed editing pass
but also I kind of just want it to be perfect
this was the original for comparison
imo i think revelation works better from a 'balancing first read clarity vs being archaic' but man is it tough to choose 😭
